docs.title: Contagion
docs.text:
The Contagion mod adds a zombie infection mechanic to PvZ Overgrowth. When a zombie hits you, there is a chance you get infected - and if the timer runs out, you die instantly. This guide explains how infection works and how to cure it.

Contagion on CurseForge

docs.title: Getting Infected
docs.text: When a zombie hits you there is an 80% chance you get infected. Armor lowers this chance by up to 60% (with full Netherite). Once infected, a timer starts counting down - if it reaches zero, you die instantly.

docs.title: Protection HUD
docs.text: A small HUD display shows your current protection value against infections. For example, a value of 20% means roughly 8 out of 10 zombie hits will infect you. The display can be enabled/disabled and repositioned in the client config settings.

docs.title: Curing the Infection
docs.text: There are multiple ways to deal with the infection, from a quick fix to a full cure.

docs.title: Quick Fix: Drink Milk
docs.text: On our server, drinking milk will immediately stop the infection. This is the easiest option if you're in a pinch and have a bucket handy.

docs.title: Step 1: Get Contagious Flesh
docs.text: Kill zombies - there is a 7.5% chance they drop Contagious Flesh. The Looting enchantment adds 2.5% per level.

docs.title: Step 2: Smelt into Heated Zombie Flesh
docs.text: Cook the Contagious Flesh in a furnace to get Heated Zombie Flesh, which is no longer contagious.

docs.title: Step 3: Craft Gold Streaked Zombie Flesh
docs.text: Combine the Heated Zombie Flesh with Gold Ingots to get Gold Streaked Zombie Flesh. Eating it resets the infection timer, but does not cure you.

docs.title: Step 4: Brew The Cure
docs.text: Combine Gold Streaked Zombie Flesh with an Awkward Potion in a brewing stand to get The Cure.

docs.title: Step 5 (Optional): Extend the Cure
docs.text: Add Redstone to The Cure to create Cure Potion II, which lasts 3x as long.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Method Effect Milk Stops infection immediately (server only) Gold Streaked Flesh Resets timer, does NOT cure The Cure Fully cures infection Cure Potion II Fully cures, lasts 3x longer

docs.title: Plant Spawn
docs.text:
Plants spawn naturally when chunks first load based on biome conditions. Unlike mobs, they do not respawn continuously and generate only once per chunk during terrain creation.

  • Already-explored chunks won't generate new plants
  • Rarer plants appear only in particular biomes
  • Exploration is essential for progression

docs.title: Effects
docs.text:
Status effects in Archipelago Additions that apply to dragons or players.

Effect Type Summary Blue Oleander Negative Damage over time to dragons, scales up Bonded Positive Coming in 2.5.0 Dragon Blood Scent Negative Prevents interacting with dragons Dragon Nip Positive Makes dragons passive, allows taming Dragon Root Negative Makes dragons hostile to each other Grimora Toxin Negative Coming in 2.5.0 Refined Dragon Root Negative Grounds dragons, dismounts riders Wild Aggression Negative Coming in 2.5.0

docs.title: Blue Oleander
docs.text:
Inflicts damage over time to dragons. Acts like the Wither Effect but slower, only harms dragons, and scales in damage the longer it's active. Cure by right-clicking an afflicted dragon with Scauldron Venom.

Immune dragons: Flightmare, Shockjaw, Scauldron, Skrill, Skrillcutter, Skrillknapper

When a dragon gets the effect, they take 4 damage (2 hearts) initially. Every 10 minutes, a damage tick occurs that increases by 50% compound each time. Eventually the damage will one-shot the dragon.

Cause Duration Notes Blue Oleander (block) 23:36:40 Only when in range, difficulty above Peaceful Concentrated Blue Oleander Poison 1:20:00 Splash Poison of Concentrated Blue Oleander 60:00 Lingering Poison of Concentrated Blue Oleander 20:00 Concentrated Blue Oleander Arrow 10:00

docs.title: Dragon Blood Scent
docs.text:
Prevents a player from directly interacting with dragons for the duration (typically 30 minutes). Cannot be removed with milk or commands - only dying or waiting it out.

Players cannot command, ride, or tame any dragons while afflicted. Indirectly commanding dragons with Dragon Command Items bypasses this effect.

Cause Duration Notes Killed a Dragon 30:00

docs.title: Dragon Nip
docs.text:
Makes affected dragons passive and allows taming wild dragons without meeting their taming requirements (correct food item still required).

Cause Duration Notes Dragon Nip (item) 00:15 Right-click on a dragon Dragon Nip Crop 00:10 When in range Dragon Nip Grass 00:10 When in range

docs.title: Dragon Root
docs.text:
Makes affected dragons hostile towards other nearby susceptible dragons. Cure by right-clicking with a Dragon Root Remedy.

Immune dragons: Crimson Goregutter, Gronckle, Hotburple, Rumblehorn, Scuttleclaw, Thunderbottom

Cause Duration Notes Dragon Root Crop 00:25 In range, difficulty above Peaceful Dragon Rooted Dirt 00:50 Standing on the block, difficulty above Peaceful Wild Dragon Root 00:50 Standing on the block, difficulty above Peaceful

docs.title: Refined Dragon Root
docs.text:
Forces dragons to land, cripples movement, and kicks riders off. Prevents remounting until cured. Wild dragons under this effect can be fed without meeting taming requirements, but can't be ridden. Cure with Dragon Root Remedy.

Immune dragons: Crimson Goregutter, Gronckle, Hotburple, Rumblehorn, Scuttleclaw, Thunderbottom

Cause Duration Notes Caged Dragons (structures) Very long Applied on generation Refined Dragon Root Arrow 10:00 Refined Dragon Root Potion 1:20:00 Creative only Splash Potion of Refined Dragon Root 60:00 Creative only Lingering Potion of Refined Dragon Root 20:00 Creative only
